option.name: fileSummaryMaps
option.value: {"C:/Users/Nilesh Sawant/AndroidStudioProjects/SchoolBusApp":"{\"/IMPLEMENTATION_CHECKLIST.md\":\"This file is an implementation checklist for the \\\"Stop Notification Feature\\\" in the application. It covers the completed items, files created/modified, code quality, configuration, deployment steps, security, device compatibility, next steps, support resources, feature highlights, learning outcomes, and the final verification status. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather serves as a comprehensive documentation and tracking tool for the implementation of this feature.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/AdminDashboardActivity.kt\":\"This file contains the implementation of the `AdminDashboardActivity` class, which is responsible for displaying the admin dashboard of a school bus application.\\n\\n`AdminDashboardActivity`: This class represents the admin dashboard activity, where the admin can view the total number of students, buses, and active buses, as well as navigate to various other activities such as adding students, buses, managing routes, and fee plans.\\n\\n`loadDashboardCountsRealtime()`: This function is responsible for loading the real-time counts of students, buses, and active buses from the Firebase Firestore and Firebase Realtime Database, and updating the corresponding TextViews in the dashboard.\\n\\n`onDestroy()`: This function is overridden to remove the listeners for the students, buses, and active buses data when the activity is destroyed.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/ManageFeePlansActivity.kt\":\"This file contains a single class, `ManageFeePlansActivity`, which is responsible for managing fee plans in a school bus application. The file also includes several functions that handle the various operations related to fee plans, such as loading, adding, editing, and deleting fee plans.\\n\\n`ManageFeePlansActivity`: This class is responsible for managing the fee plans in the school bus application, including displaying a list of fee plans, adding new fee plans, and allowing users to edit or delete existing fee plans.\\n\\n`loadFeePlans()`: This function loads the fee plans from the Firebase Firestore database and updates the list of fee plans displayed in the RecyclerView.\\n\\n`showAddFeePlanDialog()`: This function displays a dialog for adding a new fee plan, allowing the user to enter the plan type, amount, and description.\\n\\n`showEditDeleteDialog(feePlan: FeePlan)`: This function displays a dialog that allows the user to choose whether to edit or delete a specific fee plan.\\n\\n`showEditFeePlanDialog(feePlan: FeePlan)`: This function displays a dialog for editing an existing fee plan, allowing the user to update the plan type, amount, and description.\\n\\n`showDeleteConfirmationDialog(feePlan: FeePlan)`: This function displays a confirmation dialog to ensure the user wants to delete a specific fee plan.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/Route.kt\":\"This file defines a data class `Route` that represents a school bus route. The class has three properties: `id`, `name`, and `stops`, which is a list of `Stop` objects.\\n\\n`Route`: A data class that represents a school bus route with an ID, name, and a list of stops.\",\"/FIRESTORE_RULES_FIX.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it provides a set of Firestore rules that define the access control and security policies for a Firestore database. The rules cover various collections, such as users, students, buses, routes, and student attendance, and specify the read, write, and administrative permissions for each collection. The file also includes helper functions to check the user\\u0027s role (admin, driver, or parent) and a default deny-all rule for any other documents.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/FeePlanAdapter.kt\":\"This file contains a single class, `FeePlanAdapter`, which is a custom adapter for a RecyclerView that displays a list of `FeePlan` objects.\\n\\n`FeePlanAdapter`: This class is responsible for creating and binding the views for each `FeePlan` item in the RecyclerView.\\n\\n`onCreateViewHolder`: This function creates a new `FeePlanViewHolder` instance and inflates the `item_fee_plan` layout.\\n\\n`onBindViewHolder`: This function binds the data from a `FeePlan` object to the views in the `FeePlanViewHolder`.\\n\\n`getItemCount`: This function returns the number of `FeePlan` objects in the list.\\n\\n`FeePlanViewHolder`: This class holds the references to the views in the `item_fee_plan` layout.\",\"/FIRESTORE_RULES_FINAL.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it provides the correct structure and format for Firestore rules in Firebase. The file includes the following:\\n\\n1. A set of helper functions defined at the service level:\\n - `isAdmin(uid)`: Checks if the user with the given `uid` has the \\u0027admin\\u0027 role.\\n - `isDriver(uid)`: Checks if the user with the given `uid` has the \\u0027driver\\u0027 role.\\n - `isParent(uid)`: Checks if the user with the given `uid` has the \\u0027parent\\u0027 role.\\n\\n2. A set of rules that define the access permissions for different collections in the Firestore database, such as:\\n - `users`: Allows read access for all authenticated users, create access for self-registration, and update/delete access for admins and the user themselves.\\n - `students`, `buses`, and `routes`: Allows read access for all authenticated users, and create/update/delete access for admins only.\\n - `student_attendance`: Allows read access for all authenticated users, and create/update/delete access for drivers and admins.\\n - A default rule that denies all read and write access.\\n\\nThe file provides a well-structured and secure set of Firestore rules that can be used as a starting point for a Firebase-based application.\",\"/LOCATION_DEBUGGING_GUIDE.md\":\"This file is a debugging guide for location updates in an Android app. It provides step-by-step instructions and troubleshooting tips to help developers identify and resolve issues related to location tracking and data synchronization with Firebase. The guide does not contain any specific class or function definitions, but rather outlines the overall process and common problems that may arise during the development and testing of the location update feature.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/DriverRouteMapActivity.kt\":\"This file contains the implementation of the `DriverRouteMapActivity` class, which is responsible for displaying the driver\\u0027s route and stops on a Google Map. Here\\u0027s a summary of the classes and functions in the file:\\n\\n`DriverRouteMapActivity`: This class extends `AppCompatActivity` and implements the `OnMapReadyCallback` interface to handle the initialization and display of the Google Map.\\n\\n`loadDriverRouteAndStops()`: This function retrieves the driver\\u0027s assigned bus and the corresponding route details from Firestore, and then displays the route and stops on the map.\\n\\n`displayRoute(route: Route)`: This function takes a `Route` object and adds markers for each stop, draws a smooth polyline connecting the stops, and centers the map on the route.\\n\\n`loadDriverLocation(map: GoogleMap, stops: List\\u003cStop\\u003e)`: This function retrieves the driver\\u0027s current location from the Realtime Database and adds a marker for the driver\\u0027s location, as well as a dotted polyline from the driver\\u0027s location to the first stop.\\n\\n`checkProximityToStops(driverLocation: LatLng, stops: List\\u003cStop\\u003e)`: This function checks if the driver is within a proximity radius of any of the stops and notifies the user if a stop is reached.\\n\\n`redrawPolylines(map: GoogleMap, driverLocation: LatLng, stops: List\\u003cStop\\u003e)`: This function redraws the polylines on the map to show the driver\\u0027s progress along the route.\\n\\n`drawDottedPolylineWithObject(map: GoogleMap, start: LatLng, end: LatLng)`: This function draws a dotted polyline between two `LatLng` points.\\n\\n`drawSmoothPolylineWithObject(map: GoogleMap, stops: List\\u003cStop\\u003e)`: This function draws a smooth polyline connecting a list of `Stop` objects.\\n\\n`calculateDistance(location1: LatLng, location2: LatLng)`: This function calculates the distance between two `LatLng` points.\\n\\n`drawDottedPolyline(map: GoogleMap, start: LatLng, end: LatLng)`: This function draws a dotted polyline between two `LatLng` points.\\n\\n`drawSmoothPolyline(map: GoogleMap, stops: List\\u003cStop\\u003e)`: This function draws a smooth polyline connecting a list of `Stop` objects.\\n\\n`getIntermediatePoints(start: LatLng, end: LatLng, numPoints: Int)`: This function generates a list of intermediate `LatLng` points between two `LatLng` points for a smooth curve.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/BoardingStudentAdapter.kt\":\"This file contains a single class and several functions:\\n\\n`BoardingStudentAdapter`: This class is a custom adapter for a RecyclerView that displays a list of boarding students, allowing the user to mark them as boarded or dropped.\\n\\n`formatTime`: This function takes a timestamp and formats it as a time string in the format \\\"h:mm a\\\".\\n\\nThis file is responsible for providing a custom adapter for a RecyclerView that displays a list of boarding students, with the ability to mark them as boarded or dropped. The adapter handles the visual representation of the students, including their name, class, attendance status, and boarding/dropping times. It also provides click listeners for the \\\"Boarded\\\" and \\\"Dropped\\\" buttons, allowing the user to update the status of each student.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/AddBusActivity.kt\":\"This file contains a single class, `AddBusActivity`, which is responsible for handling the addition of a new bus to a Firebase Firestore database.\\n\\n`AddBusActivity`: This class extends `AppCompatActivity` and provides a user interface for adding a new bus to the Firestore database.\\n\\n`onCreate`: This function initializes the activity, sets up the toolbar, and binds the UI elements to the corresponding variables.\\n\\n`saveBus`: This function is called when the user clicks the \\\"Save Bus\\\" button. It retrieves the input values from the UI, validates them, and then saves the bus data to the Firestore database.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/DriverDashboardActivity.kt\":\"This file contains the implementation of the `DriverDashboardActivity` class, which is responsible for the driver\\u0027s dashboard in a school bus application.\\n\\n`DriverDashboardActivity`: This class represents the driver\\u0027s dashboard, where the driver can start and stop sharing their live location, manage boarding, and view the route.\\n\\n`hasLocationPermission()`: This function checks if the app has the necessary location permission granted.\\n\\n`requestLocationPermission()`: This function requests the location permission from the user.\\n\\n`startSharingService()`: This function starts the location sharing service and updates the corresponding flag in the Firebase database.\\n\\n`stopSharingService()`: This function stops the location sharing service and updates the corresponding flag in the Firebase database.\\n\\n`loadAssignedBus()`: This function retrieves the bus assigned to the current driver from the Firebase Firestore and updates the UI accordingly.\\n\\n`onRequestPermissionsResult()`: This function handles the result of the location permission request.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/PaymentActivity.kt\":\"This file is an Android app activity called `PaymentActivity` that handles the payment process for a school bus fee. It interacts with Firebase Firestore to retrieve student information and fee plans, and uses the Razorpay payment gateway to process the payment. The activity also saves the payment record and updates the student\\u0027s fee status in the database.\\n\\n`PaymentActivity`: An activity that handles the payment process for a school bus fee.\\n\\n`loadStudentInfo()`: Loads the student information from the Firebase Firestore database.\\n\\n`loadFeePlans()`: Loads the available fee plans from the Firebase Firestore database and populates the radio group.\\n\\n`updateAmountDisplay()`: Updates the display of the amount to be paid based on the selected fee plan.\\n\\n`startPayment()`: Initiates the payment process using the Razorpay payment gateway.\\n\\n`onPaymentSuccess(razorpayPaymentId: String?)`: Handles the successful payment and saves the payment record, updates the student\\u0027s fee status, and navigates back to the dashboard.\\n\\n`onPaymentError(code: Int, response: String?)`: Handles the payment error and saves the failed payment record.\\n\\n`savePaymentRecord(razorpayPaymentId: String?, status: String)`: Saves the payment record to the Firebase Firestore database.\\n\\n`updateStudentFeeStatus()`: Updates the student\\u0027s fee status in the Firebase Firestore database.\",\"/AGENTS.md\":\"This file appears to be a set of guidelines for an AI coding agent working on the SchoolBusApp, a Kotlin Android app for school bus management. It provides an overview of the app\\u0027s architecture, key patterns, developer workflows, and integration points. There are no specific classes or functions listed, but the file summarizes the following:\\n\\n`SchoolBusApp`: A Kotlin Android app for school bus management with role-based access (Admin, Driver, Parent).\\n\\n`Architecture Overview`: Describes the key components of the app, including Activities, Services, Data Models, and Adapters.\\n\\n`Key Patterns`: Outlines the role-based navigation, realtime updates, location sharing, data storage, and UI conventions used in the app.\\n\\n`Developer Workflows`: Provides guidance on building, running/debugging, testing, and setting up Firebase for the app.\\n\\n`Integration Points`: Discusses the integration with Google Maps API, permissions, and external dependencies like Firebase and Google Play Services.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/LocationForegroundService.kt\":\"This file contains a single class, `LocationForegroundService`, which is a service that runs in the foreground and updates the location of a school bus on a Firebase database.\\n\\n`LocationForegroundService`: A service that runs in the foreground and updates the location of a school bus on a Firebase database.\\n\\n`onCreate()`: Initializes the service, creates a notification channel, and starts the foreground service.\\n\\n`onStartCommand(Intent?, Int, Int)`: Starts the location updates and updates the Firebase database with the bus\\u0027s location.\\n\\n`startLocationUpdates()`: Starts requesting location updates from the device\\u0027s location services and updates the Firebase database with the bus\\u0027s location.\\n\\n`stopLocationUpdates()`: Stops requesting location updates from the device\\u0027s location services.\\n\\n`buildNotification(String)`: Builds a notification to be displayed in the foreground service.\\n\\n`createNotificationChannel()`: Creates a notification channel for the foreground service.\\n\\n`onDestroy()`: Stops the location updates, updates the Firebase database to indicate that the bus is no longer sharing its location, and stops the service.\\n\\n`onBind(Intent?)`: Returns `null` as this service is not intended to be bound to.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/StopDetectionManager.kt\":\"This file contains a single class and several functions:\\n\\n`StopDetectionManager`: A class that manages the detection of bus stops and sends notifications to parents when the bus reaches a stop.\\n\\n`checkAndNotifyStopsReached`: Checks if the driver is near any stop and sends notifications to parents if a stop is reached.\\n`sendNotificationsToParents`: Sends notifications to all parents whose children are on the bus when a stop is reached.\\n`resetVisitedStops`: Resets the list of visited stops, typically called when the driver starts a new route or day.\\n`calculateDistance`: Calculates the distance between two locations in meters.\\n`hasReachedStop`: Checks if the driver has reached a specific stop.\\n\\nThis file is responsible for the stop detection and notification functionality in the School Bus App, ensuring that parents are informed when the bus reaches a stop along the route.\",\"/CHANGES_MADE.md\":\"This document does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it provides a detailed overview of the changes made to the codebase to debug a location update issue. The key points covered in the document are:\\n\\n1. The modified files and the changes made to them, including the addition of detailed logging to help with debugging.\\n2. The creation of new documentation files that provide an overview of the issue, a root cause analysis, troubleshooting steps, and a quick start guide.\\n3. An explanation of how the added logging helps in the debugging process, including the ability to compare the bus IDs used by the driver and the parent.\\n4. The expected log output when everything works correctly and when there is a bus ID mismatch.\\n5. The next steps to be taken, including following the action plan, verifying the logs, and comparing the bus IDs.\\n\\nOverall, this document serves as a comprehensive guide to the location update debugging process, providing a clear and structured approach to identifying and resolving the issue.\",\"/EXACT_LOGS_GUIDE.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a detailed guide on how to test and debug a location-sharing feature in an Android app. The file provides instructions on what logs to look for in the Android Studio Logcat, how to identify and resolve common issues, and a pre-testing checklist. The guide aims to help developers ensure that the location-sharing functionality is working correctly between the driver\\u0027s and parent\\u0027s apps.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/ParentNotificationAdapter.kt\":\"`ParentNotificationAdapter`: A RecyclerView adapter that displays a list of parent notifications and provides a way to dismiss them.\\n\\n`onCreateViewHolder`: Creates a new `NotificationViewHolder` instance for each item in the list.\\n\\n`onBindViewHolder`: Binds the data for a specific notification to the corresponding `NotificationViewHolder`.\\n\\n`getItemCount`: Returns the number of notifications in the list.\\n\\n`removeNotification`: Removes a specific notification from the list and updates the RecyclerView.\\n\\n`formatTime`: Formats the timestamp of a notification to a readable time string.\\n\\n`NotificationViewHolder`: A ViewHolder class that holds the views for a single notification item.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/ViewStudentsActivity.kt\":\"This file is the implementation of the `ViewStudentsActivity` class, which is responsible for displaying a list of students and providing functionality to search, edit, and delete students.\\n\\n`ViewStudentsActivity`: This class is responsible for displaying a list of students, allowing the user to search for students, and providing functionality to edit and delete students.\\n\\n`loadStudents()`: This function retrieves the list of students from the Firestore database and populates the `allStudents` and `filteredStudents` lists.\\n\\n`filterStudents(query: String)`: This function filters the list of students based on the search query entered by the user.\\n\\n`updateEmptyState(query: String)`: This function updates the visibility of the RecyclerView and the empty state TextView based on the filtered list of students.\\n\\n`toggleFeeStatus(student: StudentFee)`: This function updates the fee status of a student in the Firestore database.\\n\\n`openEditStudent(student: StudentFee)`: This function opens the `EditStudentActivity` with the selected student\\u0027s information.\\n\\n`showDeleteConfirmationDialog(student: StudentFee)`: This function displays a confirmation dialog before deleting a student.\\n\\n`deleteStudent(student: StudentFee)`: This function deletes a student from the Firestore database.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/ParentNotification.kt\":\"This file defines a data class `ParentNotification` that represents a notification sent to a parent in a school bus application.\\n\\n`ParentNotification`: A data class that represents a notification sent to a parent, containing an ID, text, read status, and timestamp.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions, only the definition of the `ParentNotification` data class.\",\"/settings.gradle.kts\":\"This file is a Gradle configuration file for an Android project called \\\"SchoolBusApp\\\". It sets up the plugin management and dependency resolution for the project, specifying the repositories to be used and the rules for resolving dependencies.\\n\\n`pluginManagement`: This block configures the plugin repositories to be used, including Google, Maven Central, and the Gradle Plugin Portal.\\n\\n`dependencyResolutionManagement`: This block configures the dependency resolution settings, including the repositories to be used and the behavior when project-specific repositories are encountered.\\n\\n`rootProject.name`: This sets the name of the root project to \\\"SchoolBusApp\\\".\\n\\n`include(\\\":app\\\")`: This includes the \\\":app\\\" module in the project.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/EditStudentActivity.kt\":\"This file is an Android Kotlin code for an activity called `EditStudentActivity` that allows the user to edit the details of a student in a school bus app.\\n\\n`EditStudentActivity`: This class is responsible for handling the editing of a student\\u0027s information, including their name, parent email, class, bus ID, fee plan, and fee status.\\n\\n`onCreate`: This function initializes the activity, sets up the UI elements, and populates the student\\u0027s information based on the data passed from the previous activity.\\n\\n`updateStudent`: This function is called when the user clicks the \\\"Update Student\\\" button. It retrieves the updated student information from the UI elements, checks if the parent is registered, and then updates the student\\u0027s information in the Firestore database.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/FeeModels.kt\":\"This file defines two data classes:\\n\\n`FeePlan`: A data class that represents a fee plan, including its ID, type, amount, and description.\\n\\n`Payment`: A data class that represents a payment, including the student ID, parent UID, amount, plan type, timestamp, status, transaction ID, and Razorpay-specific details.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions, but rather defines the structure of the `FeePlan` and `Payment` data classes, which can be used throughout the application to represent and manage fee plans and payments.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/Bus.kt\":\"This file defines a data class `Bus` that represents a school bus. The class has the following properties:\\n\\n`Bus`: A data class that represents a school bus with properties such as `id`, `busId`, `route`, `driverEmail`, and `routeId`.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any functions, but rather defines the structure of the `Bus` data class, which can be used to represent and work with school bus information in the application.\",\"/REALTIME_DATABASE_RULES.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a guide for setting up security rules for a Realtime Database in a Firebase project. The guide covers the current issue, root cause, and a step-by-step solution to set the appropriate security rules in the Firebase console. It also provides important notes, explains why the solution works, and includes a verification checklist and troubleshooting steps.\",\"/FIREBASE_LOCATION_CHECKLIST.md\":\"This file is a verification checklist for Firebase Location Tracking. It provides a step-by-step guide to ensure the correct setup and functioning of the location tracking feature in a mobile application. The file covers critical aspects such as bus ID matching, Realtime Database rules, verification steps, troubleshooting, and a testing checklist. It does not contain any specific class or function definitions, but rather provides a comprehensive overview of the necessary setup and validation process for the location tracking functionality.\",\"/app/src/androidTest/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/ExampleInstrumentedTest.kt\":\"This file contains an instrumented test for the \\\"SchoolBusApp\\\" Android application. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather a single test case that checks if the application\\u0027s package name matches the expected value.\\n\\n`ExampleInstrumentedTest`: This is an instrumented test class that runs on an Android device and tests the application\\u0027s context.\\n\\n`useAppContext()`: This test function checks if the application\\u0027s package name matches the expected value, which is \\\"com.example.schoolbusapp\\\".\",\"/ACTION_PLAN.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is an immediate action plan that provides a step-by-step guide to troubleshoot an issue where location updates from a real mobile device are not showing on a parent\\u0027s map. The plan includes checking the Firebase console, verifying bus assignments, rebuilding and testing the app, checking logs, and identifying the root cause of the issue. The file also includes a checklist, estimated time, and expected outcomes to help resolve the problem.\",\"/README_FIRST.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a README file that provides a comprehensive overview of the \\\"Stop Notification Feature\\\" implementation, including the following:\\n\\n1. A quick start guide with step-by-step instructions for different roles (Project Manager, Developer, QA/Tester, Architect).\\n2. A detailed list of all the files created and modified, along with their contents.\\n3. A summary of the key features implemented, including automatic detection, smart notifications, and production-ready code.\\n4. Detailed documentation guides for each aspect of the implementation, including build instructions, testing, and deployment.\\n5. A quality assurance section that covers code quality, documentation, and testing.\\n6. A feature status summary and a final checklist to ensure the implementation is complete and ready for use.\\n7. Guidance on how to get started and troubleshoot any issues that may arise.\",\"/STOP_NOTIFICATION_FEATURE.md\":\"This file is an implementation guide for the \\\"Stop Notification Feature\\\" in the school bus tracking application. It describes the new classes and functions added, as well as the overall data flow and testing checklist. The file does not contain any specific class or function definitions, but rather provides a high-level overview of the feature implementation.\\n\\n`NotificationHelper.kt`: A utility class that handles creating and sending notifications to parents when the school bus reaches a designated stop.\\n\\n`StopDetectionManager.kt`: A core manager class that detects when the driver reaches stops and triggers notifications to the parents.\\n\\n`DriverRouteMapActivity.kt`: The modified version of the activity that integrates the stop detection and notification functionality.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/StudentFeeAdapter.kt\":\"This file contains a single class, `StudentFeeAdapter`, which is a custom adapter for a `RecyclerView` that displays a list of `StudentFee` objects. The adapter provides functionality to toggle the fee status, edit, and delete student fee information.\\n\\n`StudentFeeAdapter`: A custom adapter for a `RecyclerView` that displays a list of `StudentFee` objects and provides functionality to toggle the fee status, edit, and delete student fee information.\\n\\n`onCreateViewHolder`: Creates a new `VH` instance and inflates the `item_student_fee` layout.\\n\\n`getItemCount`: Returns the size of the `list` of `StudentFee` objects.\\n\\n`onBindViewHolder`: Binds the data from the `StudentFee` object at the given position to the views in the `VH` instance.\",\"/FIRESTORE_RULES_SETUP.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it provides a detailed guide on how to set up Firestore security rules for a school bus app\\u0027s \\\"Route \\u0026 Stops\\\" feature. The guide covers the current issue, the solution, the steps to update the security rules, the changes made, and how to test the fix. It also includes information about the expected Firestore structure after the fix and troubleshooting steps. Finally, it outlines the next steps to be taken after the routes are working, such as testing route assignment to buses, verifying route data persistence, preparing the driver dashboard, and integrating the map.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/MainActivity.kt\":\"This file appears to be an Android application\\u0027s `MainActivity` class, which is responsible for the main user interface of the application.\\n\\n`MainActivity`: The main activity class that sets up the application\\u0027s layout and handles window insets to ensure the UI is properly positioned on the screen.\\n\\n`onCreate`: The function that is called when the activity is created, where the activity\\u0027s layout is set, edge-to-edge mode is enabled, and a window insets listener is set up to adjust the UI padding based on the system bars.\",\"/DOCUMENTATION_INDEX.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a comprehensive documentation index that provides an overview of the various documentation files available for a project. The file covers the following:\\n\\n1. Starting points for different roles (project managers, developers, QA, architects)\\n2. Detailed descriptions of each documentation file, including the target audience, duration, and contents\\n3. Relationships between the documentation files\\n4. Comparison of the documentation files based on length, technicality, and practicality\\n5. Quick lookup guide for finding answers to common questions\\n6. Key topics covered in each documentation file\\n7. Recommended reading workflow and learning path\\n8. Documentation statistics and quality metrics\\n9. Notes for readers about the documentation\\n10. Next steps for different usage scenarios\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to serve as a central index and guide to the complete documentation for the project, making it easy for users to navigate and find the information they need based on their role and requirements.\",\"/app/google-services.json\":\"This file appears to be a JSON configuration file for a Firebase project. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather provides information about the project, such as the project number, project ID, and storage bucket. The file also includes client information, such as the mobile SDK app ID and the Android client package name. Additionally, it includes API key information and details about the app invite service. Overall, this file is used to configure the Firebase project settings for the \\\"SchoolBusApp\\\" application.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/ManageBoardingActivity.kt\":\"This file is an Android app activity that manages the boarding and dropping of students on a school bus. It uses Firebase Firestore to store and retrieve student attendance data.\\n\\n`ManageBoardingActivity`: This class is responsible for managing the boarding and dropping of students on a school bus.\\n\\n`loadStudentsForBus()`: This function loads the list of students associated with the current bus.\\n\\n`loadTodayAttendanceStatuses()`: This function loads the attendance status (boarded, dropped) for each student for the current day.\\n\\n`markBoarded(student: BoardingStudent, showToast: Boolean \\u003d true)`: This function marks a student as boarded and updates the attendance data in Firestore.\\n\\n`markDropped(student: BoardingStudent, showToast: Boolean \\u003d true, onComplete: (() -\\u003e Unit)? \\u003d null)`: This function marks a student as dropped and updates the attendance data in Firestore.\\n\\n`getTodayDate()`: This function returns the current date in the format \\\"yyyy-MM-dd\\\".\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/ViewBusesActivity.kt\":\"This file is an Android Kotlin code for a school bus app that allows users to view and manage buses. It includes the following classes and functions:\\n\\n`ViewBusesActivity`: This class is the main activity that displays a list of buses and allows the user to search, edit, and delete buses.\\n\\n`loadBuses()`: This function retrieves the list of buses from the Firebase Firestore database and populates the `allBuses` and `filteredBuses` lists.\\n\\n`filterBuses(query: String)`: This function filters the list of buses based on the user\\u0027s search query and updates the `filteredBuses` list accordingly.\\n\\n`updateEmptyState(query: String)`: This function updates the visibility of the \\\"No buses available\\\" or \\\"No buses match your search\\\" message based on the filtered buses list and the search query.\\n\\n`showDeleteConfirmationDialog(bus: Bus)`: This function displays a confirmation dialog to the user before deleting a bus.\\n\\n`deleteBus(bus: Bus)`: This function deletes the specified bus from the Firebase Firestore database and the Firebase Realtime Database.\",\"/FILES_CREATED_MODIFIED.md\":\"This file is a comprehensive documentation of the changes made to the SchoolBusApp project, including the implementation of a new stop notification feature. It provides a detailed overview of the project structure, the files created and modified, and the overall implementation details. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather serves as a reference guide for the project stakeholders.\\n\\nThe key sections in the file are:\\n\\n1. **Project Structure**: Provides a visual representation of the project structure after the implementation of the new feature.\\n2. **File Statistics**: Summarizes the new files created, the modified files, and the overall changes in terms of lines of code and documentation.\\n3. **Detailed File Descriptions**: Provides a detailed description of each new and modified file, including their purpose, key functions, and features.\\n4. **What Changed \\u0026 What Didn\\u0027t**: Highlights the files that remained unchanged, the new files, and the modified files, along with the impact of the changes.\\n5. **Code Metrics**: Presents the overall statistics of the new and modified code, as well as the documentation.\\n6. **Dependency Check**: Ensures that no new external dependencies were added to the project.\\n7. **Integration Points**: Identifies the integration points where the new code interacts with the existing codebase.\\n8. **Cleanup Notes**: Provides instructions for removing the new feature if needed.\\n9. **Verification Checklist**: Confirms that all the necessary steps have been completed for the implementation.\\n10. **File Reading Order**: Suggests the recommended order for reviewing the files.\",\"/DIRECTIONS_API_SETUP.md\":\"This file is a Google Directions API setup guide that provides instructions on how to enable the Directions API for a Google Cloud project. It does not contain any classes or functions. The file outlines the steps to enable the Directions API, troubleshoot common errors, and use a fallback option if the API still fails to work.\",\"/LOCATION_UPDATE_FIX_INDEX.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a comprehensive documentation guide that provides step-by-step instructions and troubleshooting information for resolving a location update issue in a mobile application. The guide includes various sections such as the issue description, root cause analysis, documentation files created, quick start guide, detailed process, code changes made, log formats to look for, success checklist, common issues and solutions, file organization, time estimates, related documentation, and next steps. The goal of this documentation is to help the user quickly identify and fix the location update issue in the mobile application.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/AddStudentActivity.kt\":\"This file is an Android Kotlin code for an activity called `AddStudentActivity` that allows the user to add a new student to a school bus app. The activity interacts with a Firebase Firestore database to store the student\\u0027s information.\\n\\n`AddStudentActivity`: This class is responsible for handling the user interface and logic for adding a new student to the school bus app.\\n\\n`onCreate`: This function is called when the activity is created, and it sets up the user interface, including the toolbar, input fields, and buttons. It also sets up the click listener for the \\\"Save Student\\\" button.\\n\\n`btnSaveStudent.setOnClickListener`: This function is called when the user clicks the \\\"Save Student\\\" button. It retrieves the user input, validates it, and then saves the student\\u0027s information to the Firebase Firestore database.\",\"/app/src/test/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/ExampleUnitTest.kt\":\"This file contains a single unit test class with one test function. The file does not define any classes or functions, but rather contains a single unit test that checks if the addition operation `2 + 2` is correct.\\n\\n`ExampleUnitTest`: A unit test class that contains a single test function to verify the correctness of a simple addition operation.\\n\\n`addition_isCorrect()`: A test function that checks if the addition operation `2 + 2` returns the expected result of `4`.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/PaymentHistoryAdapter.kt\":\"`PaymentHistoryAdapter`: A RecyclerView adapter that displays a list of payment history items.\\n\\n`onCreateViewHolder`: Creates a new `PaymentViewHolder` instance for each item in the payment history list.\\n\\n`onBindViewHolder`: Binds the payment data to the corresponding views in the `PaymentViewHolder`.\\n\\n`getItemCount`: Returns the number of items in the payment history list.\\n\\n`formatDate`: Formats the timestamp of a payment to a human-readable date and time string.\\n\\n`PaymentViewHolder`: A ViewHolder class that holds references to the views in the payment history item layout.\\n\\nThis file contains the implementation of a `PaymentHistoryAdapter` class, which is responsible for displaying a list of payment history items in a RecyclerView. The adapter handles the creation and binding of view holders, as well as the formatting of payment data such as the plan type, amount, date, and status.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/EditRouteActivity.kt\":\"This file contains the implementation of the `EditRouteActivity` class, which is responsible for allowing the user to edit a bus route in a school bus application.\\n\\n`EditRouteActivity`: This class handles the functionality for editing a bus route, including loading the existing route, adding and removing stops, and saving the updated route to the Firebase Firestore database.\\n\\n`loadRoute()`: This function retrieves the details of the selected route from the Firebase Firestore database and populates the UI with the route name and stops.\\n\\n`showAddStopDialog()`: This function displays a dialog for the user to add a new stop to the route, including fields for the stop order, name, latitude, and longitude.\\n\\n`saveRoute()`: This function updates the route information in the Firebase Firestore database with the changes made by the user, including the route name and the list of stops.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/Stop.kt\":\"This file defines a data class called `Stop` that represents a bus stop location. The class has four properties: `order`, `name`, `lat`, and `lng`.\\n\\n`Stop`: A data class that represents a bus stop location with properties for the stop\\u0027s order, name, latitude, and longitude.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/NotificationHelper.kt\":\"This file contains a single class `NotificationHelper` and several functions within it. Here\\u0027s a summary of each:\\n\\n`NotificationHelper`: A class that provides functionality to create and manage notifications in an Android application.\\n\\n`createNotificationChannels()`: A function that creates notification channels for different types of notifications, such as stop notifications and bus sharing status notifications.\\n\\n`sendStopNotification()`: A function that sends a notification when a bus reaches a specific stop, including details about the bus, stop, and student.\\n\\n`sendBusStartedSharingNotification()`: A function that sends a notification when a bus starts sharing its live location.\\n\\n`sendBusStoppedSharingNotification()`: A function that sends a notification when a bus stops sharing its live location.\\n\\n`cancelNotification()`: A function that cancels a specific notification.\\n\\n`cancelSharingNotification()`: A function that cancels the bus sharing status notification.\",\"/QUICK_REFERENCE_CARD.md\":\"This file does not contain any classes or functions. It is a quick reference card that provides a step-by-step guide to troubleshoot an issue where the location from a real mobile device does not show on the parent map, but the notification works. The file covers the most likely cause, the steps to fix the issue, common issues and quick fixes, device setup, critical checks, timeline, success criteria, and guidance on when to seek further assistance.\",\"/ANR_FIX_GUIDE.md\":\"This file is a detailed documentation of the fixes applied to an Android app to address the \\\"System is Not Responding\\\" issue. It covers the root causes, the changes made, and the techniques used to improve the app\\u0027s performance and responsiveness. The file does not contain any specific class or function definitions, but rather provides a comprehensive overview of the problem and the solutions implemented.\\n\\nThe file covers the following key points:\\n\\n1. What was fixed: The \\\"System is Not Responding\\\" issue was caused by heavy operations blocking the main UI thread, and the root causes were identified and addressed.\\n\\n2. Changes made: The file provides detailed code examples and explanations of the changes made to the `StopDetectionManager.kt` and `DriverRouteMapActivity.kt` files, including the use of background threads, thread-safe access to shared data, and posting UI updates back to the main thread.\\n\\n3. How the fixes prevent ANR: The file compares the before and after timelines, showing how the changes prevent the main thread from being blocked for extended periods, thus avoiding the \\\"System is Not Responding\\\" issue.\\n\\n4. Thread safety: The file explains how thread-safe access to shared collections was implemented using `synchronized` blocks.\\n\\n5. Performance improvements: The file provides a table comparing the performance of various operations before and after the fixes, highlighting the significant improvements in responsiveness.\\n\\n6. Testing the fix: The file outlines the steps to verify that the ANR issue has been resolved, including monitoring the logcat output and using the Android Profiler.\\n\\n7. Potential issues and solutions: The file discusses potential issues that could arise, such as too many background threads or race conditions, and provides solutions to address them.\\n\\n8. Additional optimizations: The file suggests two additional optimization options using Kotlin Coroutines and Java Executors.\\n\\n9. Verification checklist and performance targets: The file provides a checklist to verify the fixes and outlines the expected performance targets after the changes.\\n\\nOverall, this file serves as a comprehensive guide to the ANR fix, providing a detailed explanation of the problem, the solutions implemented, and the verification process.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/BoardingStudent.kt\":\"This file contains a single data class:\\n\\n`BoardingStudent`: A data class that represents a student who boards and gets off a school bus, with properties such as their ID, name, class, parent\\u0027s UID, bus ID, and whether they have boarded or dropped off, along with the timestamps for those events.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any functions. It serves as a data model for the `BoardingStudent` entity in the school bus application.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/SplashActivity.kt\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`SplashActivity`: This class represents the splash screen activity of the School Bus App, which displays the app\\u0027s logo, title, and subtitle with a fade-in animation, and then transitions to the login activity after a 2.2-second delay.\\n\\nThe file also contains the following functions:\\n\\n`onCreate`: This function is the entry point of the `SplashActivity` class, where the layout is set, the UI elements are animated, and the transition to the login activity is scheduled.\\n\\nIn summary, this file defines the splash screen activity of the School Bus App, which provides a smooth and visually appealing introduction to the app before the user is directed to the login screen.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/StopAdapter.kt\":\"`StopAdapter`: A RecyclerView adapter that displays a list of bus stops, allowing the user to delete a stop.\\n\\n`StopViewHolder`: A ViewHolder class that holds the views for a single stop item in the RecyclerView.\\n\\n`onCreateViewHolder`: Inflates the layout for a single stop item and returns a `StopViewHolder` instance.\\n\\n`onBindViewHolder`: Binds the data for a specific stop to the views in the `StopViewHolder`.\\n\\n`getItemCount`: Returns the number of stops in the list.\\n\\nThis file defines a `StopAdapter` class that extends `RecyclerView.Adapter` to display a list of bus stops in a RecyclerView. The adapter allows the user to delete a stop by providing a callback function. The `StopViewHolder` class is used to hold the views for a single stop item in the RecyclerView.\",\"/BUILD_INSTRUCTIONS.md\":\"This file contains build and deployment instructions for the SchoolBusApp project. It does not contain any classes or functions. Instead, it provides step-by-step instructions for:\\n\\n1. Verifying the pre-build checklist.\\n2. Building the project using Android Studio, Terminal/Command Line, or PowerShell.\\n3. Identifying successful build indicators and common build errors with their fixes.\\n4. Testing the app after the build, including a quick sanity test and a full test.\\n5. Building the app for release, including updating the version, building the release APK and bundle, and signing the APK/bundle.\\n6. Analyzing build performance and optimizing it.\\n7. Deploying the app for testing on an emulator or a physical device.\\n8. Performing a clean build and verifying the build success.\\n9. Troubleshooting build issues and getting help.\\n10. Finalizing the build process and preparing for testing.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/ManageRoutesActivity.kt\":\"This file contains the implementation of the `ManageRoutesActivity` class, which is responsible for managing the routes in a school bus application.\\n\\n`ManageRoutesActivity`: This class handles the display, filtering, and management of routes, including the ability to add, edit, delete, and assign routes to buses.\\n\\n`loadRoutes()`: This function retrieves the routes from the Firestore database and populates the `allRoutes` and `filteredRoutes` lists.\\n\\n`filterRoutes(query: String)`: This function filters the list of routes based on the search query entered by the user.\\n\\n`updateEmptyState(query: String)`: This function updates the visibility of the recycler view and the empty state text based on the filtered routes.\\n\\n`showDeleteConfirmationDialog(route: Route)`: This function displays a confirmation dialog before deleting a route.\\n\\n`deleteRoute(route: Route)`: This function deletes a route from the Firestore database.\\n\\n`assignRouteToBus(route: Route)`: This function loads the available buses from the Firestore database and displays a dialog to allow the user to assign the selected route to a bus.\\n\\n`assignRouteToSelectedBus(route: Route, bus: Bus)`: This function updates the Firestore database to assign the selected route to the chosen bus.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/PaymentHistoryActivity.kt\":\"This file contains the implementation of the `PaymentHistoryActivity` class, which is responsible for displaying the payment history for a user in a School Bus App.\\n\\n`PaymentHistoryActivity`: This class is responsible for displaying the payment history for the current user.\\n\\n`onCreate`: This function sets up the UI and initializes the necessary components, such as the toolbar and the RecyclerView for displaying the payment history.\\n\\n`loadPaymentHistory`: This function retrieves the payment history data from the Firebase Firestore database and populates the RecyclerView with the payment details.\",\"/app/build.gradle.kts\":\"This file is a Gradle build script for an Android application. It sets up the project configuration, including the Android SDK version, dependencies, and build types. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather configures the build process for the Android application.\\n\\nThe main components of the file are:\\n\\n`plugins`: Specifies the plugins used in the project, including the Android application plugin, Kotlin Android plugin, and Google Play Services plugin.\\n\\n`android`: Configures the Android-specific settings, such as the application ID, SDK versions, and build types.\\n\\n`compileOptions`: Sets the Java source and target compatibility to version 11.\\n\\n`kotlinOptions`: Sets the JVM target to version 11 for Kotlin code.\\n\\n`dependencies`: Specifies the external libraries and frameworks used in the project, including Firebase, Google Play Services, and various Android support libraries.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/LoginActivity.kt\":\"This file contains a single class, `LoginActivity`, which is responsible for handling the login functionality of a school bus application.\\n\\n`LoginActivity`: This class is the main entry point for the login process, where users can enter their email and password to authenticate and access the application based on their assigned role (parent, driver, or admin).\\n\\n`onStart()`: This function checks if a user is already logged in and redirects them to the appropriate dashboard activity based on their role.\\n\\n`onCreate(Bundle)`: This function sets up the login UI and handles the login process, including validating user input, authenticating with Firebase, and redirecting the user to the appropriate dashboard activity based on their role.\\n\\n`redirectUserByRole(String)`: This function retrieves the user\\u0027s role from Firestore and redirects them to the corresponding dashboard activity (ParentDashboardActivity, DriverDashboardActivity, or AdminDashboardActivity) based on their role.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/RegisterActivity.kt\":\"This file contains a single class, `RegisterActivity`, which is responsible for handling the user registration process in a school bus application.\\n\\n`RegisterActivity`: This class is responsible for handling the user registration process, including validating user input, creating a new user account in Firebase Authentication, and storing user data in Firestore.\\n\\n`onCreate`: This function is called when the activity is created. It sets up the UI elements, initializes the Firebase Authentication and Firestore instances, and sets up click listeners for the registration and login buttons.\\n\\n`btnRegister.setOnClickListener`: This function is called when the user clicks the \\\"Register\\\" button. It validates the user input, creates a new user account in Firebase Authentication, and stores the user data in Firestore. If the registration is successful, it navigates the user to the ParentDashboardActivity.\\n\\n`tvGoLogin.setOnClickListener`: This function is called when the user clicks the \\\"Go to Login\\\" text view. It starts the LoginActivity and finishes the current activity.\",\"/build.gradle.kts\":\"This file is a top-level build file that sets up common configuration options for all sub-projects or modules in the Android application. It does not contain any classes or functions, but rather sets up the following plugins:\\n\\n`alias(libs.plugins.android.application)`: Applies the Android application plugin to all sub-projects.\\n`alias(libs.plugins.kotlin.android)`: Applies the Kotlin Android plugin to all sub-projects.\\n`id(\\\"com.google.gms.google-services\\\")`: Applies the Google Services plugin to all sub-projects.\\n\\nThe purpose of this file is to centralize common configuration settings for the entire Android application, making it easier to manage and maintain the build process across multiple sub-projects or modules.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/AddRouteActivity.kt\":\"This file contains a single class and several functions:\\n\\n`AddRouteActivity`: This class is responsible for handling the user interface and logic for adding a new route to the school bus app.\\n\\n`onCreate`: This function is called when the activity is created, and it sets up the user interface and event listeners for the \\\"Save Route\\\" button.\\n\\n`saveRoute`: This function is called when the user clicks the \\\"Save Route\\\" button, and it saves the new route information to the Firebase Firestore database.\\n\\nOverall, this file provides the implementation for an activity that allows the user to add a new route to the school bus app, and it handles the necessary interactions with the Firebase Firestore database to store the route information.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/BusAdapter.kt\":\"`BusAdapter`: A RecyclerView adapter that displays a list of buses, allowing the user to edit or delete each bus.\\n\\n`onCreateViewHolder`: Creates a new `BusViewHolder` instance for each item in the list.\\n\\n`onBindViewHolder`: Binds the data for a specific bus to the corresponding views in the `BusViewHolder`.\\n\\n`getItemCount`: Returns the number of buses in the list.\\n\\n`BusViewHolder`: A ViewHolder class that holds references to the views within each item in the RecyclerView.\\n\\nThis file defines a `BusAdapter` class that extends `RecyclerView.Adapter` and is responsible for managing the display of a list of buses in a RecyclerView. The adapter provides functionality to edit and delete individual buses, and the `BusViewHolder` class is used to hold references to the views within each item in the RecyclerView.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/AdminMapActivity.kt\":\"This file is an Android application that displays a map with the locations of school buses. It uses Firebase Realtime Database and Firestore to retrieve and display the bus locations and routes.\\n\\n`AdminMapActivity`: An activity that displays a map with the locations of school buses and allows the admin to view and select different bus routes.\\n\\n`loadRoutes()`: Loads the available bus routes from Firestore and populates a spinner with the route names.\\n\\n`showRouteOnMap(route: Route)`: Displays the selected bus route on the map, including markers for each stop and a smooth polyline connecting the stops.\\n\\n`startTrackingAllBuses()`: Starts listening for updates to the bus locations in the Firebase Realtime Database and updates the markers on the map accordingly.\\n\\n`animateMarker(marker: Marker, toPosition: LatLng)`: Animates the movement of a bus marker on the map.\\n\\n`recenterAllBuses()`: Centers the map view to show all the bus markers.\",\"/STOP_NOTIFICATION_TESTING.md\":\"This file is a testing guide for the \\\"Stop Notification Feature\\\" in a school bus app. It outlines the prerequisites, test data setup, testing steps, expected behaviors, troubleshooting, performance testing, security testing, and a test report template. The guide does not contain any classes or functions, but rather provides a comprehensive overview of the testing process for the stop notification feature.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/StudentFee.kt\":\"This file contains a single data class:\\n\\n`StudentFee`: A data class that represents a student\\u0027s fee information, including their ID, name, fee plan, fee status, class, and bus ID.\\n\\nThis file does not contain any functions. It is a simple data class that serves as a model for storing and representing student fee-related information.\",\"/LOCATION_DEBUGGING_REAL_DEVICE.md\":\"This file is a debugging guide for a live location feature in a mobile app. It provides a step-by-step approach to identify and resolve issues related to the live location functionality. The guide covers various aspects, including updating Realtime Database rules, verifying logs from the driver and parent, and checking the Firebase console. It also includes a checklist of common issues and fixes, as well as recommendations for production-ready rules. The file does not contain any classes or functions, but rather provides a comprehensive guide to troubleshoot and resolve live location-related problems in the mobile app.\",\"/LOCATION_FIX_SUMMARY.md\":\"This file is a detailed summary of a location tracking issue and the steps taken to fix it. It does not contain any specific class or function definitions. Instead, it provides the following information:\\n\\n1. A description of the issue, including the expected and actual behavior.\\n2. A root cause analysis, identifying the most likely causes of the issue.\\n3. A summary of the fixes implemented, including changes to the `LocationForegroundService.kt`, `ParentDashboardActivity.kt`, and `DriverDashboardActivity.kt` files.\\n4. Step-by-step instructions for testing the fix, including how to rebuild and deploy the app, how to check the logs, and how to compare the bus IDs between the driver and parent.\\n5. Troubleshooting steps for common issues that may arise, such as a bus ID mismatch or issues with the map update.\\n6. Information about additional documentation files created to help with debugging and verifying the location tracking functionality.\\n7. The expected result after the fix is applied, including the expected log messages and the expected behavior of the map and bus status display.\\n8. Debug tips for sharing information if the issue is still not resolved.\",\"/STOP_NOTIFICATION_IMPLEMENTATION.md\":\"This file is a technical implementation summary for the \\\"Stop Notification Feature\\\" in a school bus tracking application. It covers the following:\\n\\n`NotificationHelper`: A utility class for creating and sending notifications to parents when the school bus reaches a designated stop.\\n\\n`StopDetectionManager`: The core logic for detecting when the driver reaches stops and triggering notifications to the parents.\\n\\n`DriverRouteMapActivity`: The activity that integrates the stop detection functionality into the driver\\u0027s route map view.\\n\\nThe file also includes a detailed overview of the feature, its key components, data flow, security and privacy considerations, testing checklist, deployment steps, and future enhancement ideas.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/RouteAdapter.kt\":\"`RouteAdapter`: A RecyclerView adapter that displays a list of routes, with options to edit, delete, and assign each route.\\n\\n`RouteViewHolder`: A ViewHolder class that holds the views for each item in the RecyclerView.\\n\\n`onCreateViewHolder`: Creates a new `RouteViewHolder` instance and inflates the `item_route` layout.\\n\\n`onBindViewHolder`: Binds the data for a specific route to the views in the `RouteViewHolder`.\\n\\n`getItemCount`: Returns the number of routes in the list.\\n\\nThis file defines a `RouteAdapter` class that is used to display a list of routes in a RecyclerView. The adapter provides functionality to edit, delete, and assign each route, and the `RouteViewHolder` class is used to hold the views for each item in the RecyclerView.\",\"/QUICK_REFERENCE.md\":\"This file is a quick reference guide for the \\\"Stop Notification Feature\\\" in the School Bus App. It provides an overview of the feature, including what it does, what was added, how it works, configuration options, testing instructions, and common issues and fixes. The file also includes information about the data requirements, integration points, notification preview, key settings, permissions, and the success criteria for the feature.\\n\\nThe file does not contain any specific class or function definitions. Instead, it provides a high-level overview of the feature and the necessary information for developers to understand and test it.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/ParentDashboardActivity.kt\":\"This file contains the implementation of the `ParentDashboardActivity` class, which is responsible for displaying the parent dashboard in a school bus tracking application.\\n\\n`ParentDashboardActivity`: This class is the main activity that displays the parent dashboard, including the map, student details, bus status, and notifications.\\n\\n`ParentStudent`: This data class represents a student associated with a parent.\\n\\n`ParentNotification`: This data class represents a notification for the parent.\\n\\n`loadParentStudentDetails()`: This function loads the details of the parent\\u0027s students from the Firestore database and populates the UI.\\n\\n`showStudentDetails(student: ParentStudent)`: This function updates the UI to display the details of the selected student.\\n\\n`startLiveBusTracking()`: This function starts tracking the live location of the student\\u0027s bus and updates the map and UI accordingly.\\n\\n`startAttendanceTracking()`: This function tracks the student\\u0027s attendance (boarding and drop-off times) and updates the UI.\\n\\n`startBusSharingStatusTracking()`: This function tracks the bus\\u0027s location sharing status and sends notifications when the status changes.\\n\\n`animateMarker(marker: Marker, toPosition: LatLng)`: This function animates the movement of the bus marker on the map.\\n\\n`updateBusStatusUI()`: This function updates the UI to display the current status of the bus (live or offline).\\n\\n`showNotificationsDialog()`: This function displays a dialog showing the parent\\u0027s notifications.\\n\\n`updateNotificationIcon()`: This function updates the notification icon in the toolbar.\\n\\n`addNotification(text: String, timestamp: Long)`: This function adds a new notification to the list and updates the notification icon.\\n\\n`setupNotificationListener()`: This function sets up a listener to receive real-time updates on the parent\\u0027s notifications from Firestore.\",\"/app/src/main/java/com/example/schoolbusapp/EditBusActivity.kt\":\"This file contains a single class:\\n\\n`EditBusActivity`: This class represents an activity that allows the user to edit the details of a school bus, including the bus ID, route, and driver email.\\n\\nThe file also contains the following functions:\\n\\n`onCreate()`: This function is called when the activity is created, and it sets up the UI elements and initializes the bus data.\\n\\n`updateBus()`: This function is called when the user clicks the \\\"Update Bus\\\" button, and it updates the bus data in the Firestore database.\\n\\nOverall, this file provides the implementation for an activity that allows the user to edit the details of a school bus, and it interacts with the Firestore database to update the bus information.\"}"}
